verification_method: Manual review of primary City of London, London Datastore, ONS, GOV.UK, and regulator web/PDF sources.
data_boundary: This manifest backs the local demo corpus for City of London managed housing damp and mould appointments. London-wide open datasets are included as context and deployment evidence, not as individual entitlement sources.

sources.title: Preventing damp and mould
verified_points: Residents are asked to report damp and mould immediately and include location, extent, and household vulnerability details.
The page gives the Property Services phone number and online reporting route.
The page describes target windows for damp/mould inspections, mould treatments, leak investigations, and emergency action.
The page advises residents to keep photos and dates as records.
sources.source_locator: Report damp and mould; Our approach to damp and mould; October 2025: Awaab's Law
sources.notes: City wording describes several response windows as aims. Cards must label them as target/aim windows, not guaranteed legal outcomes.

sources.title: Damp, Mould & Condensation Policy
sources.publisher: City of London Corporation, Department of Community & Children's Services, Housing Division
verified_points: The policy scope includes HRA managed homes and named almshouse trusts.
The policy aims to keep homes dry, safe, and free from damp/mould hazards.
The policy says residents reporting damp and mould should be treated with empathy and respect and the cause should not be prejudged.
Risk assessment considers severity, location, household composition, and vulnerability.
The policy includes high-risk and medium/low-risk response aims.
The policy addresses temporary accommodation, follow-up, records, compensation, and accessible communication needs.
sources.notes: Use this as the principal local policy authority for City of London managed housing.

sources.title: Housing complaints and customer feedback
verified_points: The page tells residents to report a first issue before using the formal complaint route.
The page lists information needed for a complaint.
The page defines a complaint and gives examples including failure to meet service standards or unfair treatment.
The page says residents can contact the Housing Ombudsman Service at any stage of a complaint.
sources.source_locator: Complaints; Information you need to provide; The Housing Ombudsman
sources.notes: Complaint handling timescales are not encoded here; use the linked policy/PDF if exact stage windows are needed.

sources.title: Awaab's Law: Guidance for tenants in social housing
verified_points: Awaab's Law applies to most social housing tenants.
Emergency hazards must be investigated and made safe within 24 hours.
Significant damp and mould hazards must be investigated within 10 working days and made safe within 5 working days after investigation.
Landlords must provide a written summary after investigation and offer suitable alternative accommodation if the home cannot be made safe in time.
sources.source_locator: Quick guide; What does Awaab's Law include; Step by Step Guides
sources.notes: Coverage depends on tenancy/licence status. The demo must ask the resident or officer to confirm coverage.

sources.title: Awaab's Law: Guidance for social landlords - Timeframes for repairs in the social rented sector
verified_points: The landlord guidance includes damp and mould examples involving vulnerable residents.
The guidance gives an example where damp and mould affecting a child's asthma would likely be an emergency hazard.
The guidance states emergency damp and mould action may require attendance within 24 hours.
sources.source_locator: Emergency damp and mould hazard examples
sources.notes: Use for the child-asthma demo because it is directly aligned with the scripted scenario.

sources.title: City of London Corporation (00AA) - Regulatory Judgement: 25 February 2026
sources.publisher: Regulator of Social Housing via GOV.UK
sources.jurisdiction: City of London Corporation social housing
verified_points: The regulator says City of London Corporation owns and manages around 1,900 social housing homes.
The judgement identifies serious failings in outcomes relating to repairs, maintenance, and planned improvements.
The judgement notes ongoing engagement and monitoring by the regulator.
sources.source_locator: Regulatory judgement; Background to the judgement
sources.notes: This source provides risk/context. It should not be used to state an individual resident entitlement.

sources.title: Tenant Satisfaction Measures
verified_points: The page reports 2024-2025 tenant satisfaction and landlord performance measures.
The page reports homes not meeting the Decent Homes Standard and emergency/non-emergency responsive repair completion within target timescales.
The page says the figures are gathered through tenant surveys and management information.
sources.notes: Self-published performance data. Use in source manifest and record context, not as a policy entitlement source.

sources.title: London Datastore
verified_points: The London Datastore is the GLA's open data-sharing portal for London.
The portal indexes datasets across London topics including housing, health, and communities.
sources.notes: Portal source only. Use dataset pages for claims about specific numbers.

sources.title: Housing in London - The evidence base for the Mayor's Housing Strategy
verified_points: The dataset page provides the Housing in London 2025 report and tables.
The dataset is relevant for London-wide housing stock, supply, costs, need, mobility, and decent homes context.
sources.source_locator: Housing in London 2025 report and tables
sources.notes: Use as impact/deployment evidence after downloading the tables; not used for individual policy cards.

sources.title: Disability in England and Wales, 2021
verified_points: The dataset provides Census 2021 disability estimates by long-term health problem or disability, sex, age, and deprivation.
The estimates are as at Census Day, 21 March 2021.
sources.notes: This is disability context, not a confirmed borough-level hearing impairment or BSL-user dataset.

verified_for_demo: City of London has a current damp/mould web page and versioned damp/mould policy suitable for a local RAG corpus.
The damp/mould scenario can generate source-backed cards for reporting, response windows, vulnerability triage, accessible communication, records, complaints, and temporary accommodation.
Awaab's Law tenant and landlord guidance is directly relevant to social housing damp/mould hazards in England.
needs_confirmation: Confirm the resident's tenure and whether Awaab's Law applies to the specific appointment before presenting legal timeframes as applicable to that person.
Re-check City of London contact details, web forms, and response target wording immediately before a live demo.
Download and archive complete source PDFs/HTML under the chosen licence before production ingestion.
Confirm exact London borough hearing impairment or BSL-user prevalence tables before making borough-level deployment claims.
Have a housing/legal expert review the question prompts before using them outside the hackathon demo.
safety_notes: This corpus does not provide legal advice.
Policy cards should be shown as source-backed prompts for advocacy and record keeping, not binding determinations.
Do not infer medical severity. The agent may ask the resident to report health impact and vulnerability details because the sources say these affect triage.
